title: podman 5.3.2 unable to pull alpine images (inside container)
body:
### Issue Description

Dockerfile

```
FROM --platform=linux/amd64 eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine
RUN apk add --no-cache podman file fuse-overlayfs fuse3
RUN podman pull --platform=linux/amd64 eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine

```

On the Mac (apple silicon host):
podman --version
podman version 5.4.2

Run the build using podman:
podman build --arch amd64 --os linux -f Dockerfile --no-cache


Output below:

```
podman build --arch amd64 --os linux -f Dockerfile --no-cache
STEP 1/3: FROM --platform=linux/amd64 eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine
STEP 2/3: RUN apk add --no-cache podman file fuse-overlayfs fuse3
fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.21/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.21/community/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz

(2/32) Installing file (5.46-r2)
(3/32) Installing fuse-common (3.16.2-r1)
(4/32) Installing fuse3-libs (3.16.2-r1)
(5/32) Installing fuse3 (3.16.2-r1)

Executing fuse-overlayfs-1.14-r0.post-install

* - configure subordinate user ids (/etc/subuid) and subordinate group ids (/etc/subgid),
* - mount the control groups: `rc-service cgroups start`.
*
* More information: https://wiki.alpinelinux.org/wiki/Podman
*
Executing busybox-1.37.0-r12.trigger
Executing glib-2.82.5-r0.trigger

STEP 3/3: RUN podman pull --platform=linux/amd64 eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine
time="2025-05-14T18:40:07Z" level=warning msg="\"/\" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers"
time="2025-05-14T18:40:07Z" level=warning msg="Using rootless single mapping into the namespace. This might break some images. Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for adding sub*ids if not using a network user"
Resolving "eclipse-temurin" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:994661c98e4264bf53af5a49e1f142717665bd41915c9244d1f63dad7897149f

Copying blob sha256:26fac3e9314ebcc28feb6049f712a878479f900bc5f70456ecc2fc2fa3695d30
Copying blob sha256:6d2b9ea538182f9ba83b0f571df811bbc4de7f25b42ee9eb9733ce9d5851970f

Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:f18232174bc91741fdf3da96d85011092101a032a93a388b79e99e69c2d5c870"/""/"sha256:08000c18d16dadf9553d747a58cf44023423a9ab010aab96cf263d2216b8b350": unpacking failed (error: exit status 125; output: Error: unrecognized command `podman /`

Did you mean this?
	cp
	ps
	rm

Try 'podman --help' for more information
)
Error: building at STEP "RUN podman pull --platform=linux/amd64 eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine": while running runtime: exit status 125
```


Inside the image/container the podman built by the above Dockerfile:
~ $ podman --version
podman version 5.3.2


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use M-series Mac 
2. Use podman 5.4.2
3. Use Dockerfile (reproducer) above
4. podman build --arch amd64 --os linux -f Dockerfile --no-cache
5. View output


### Describe the results you received

Trying to pull docker.io/library/eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:994661c98e4264bf53af5a49e1f142717665bd41915c9244d1f63dad7897149f
Copying blob sha256:f18232174bc91741fdf3da96d85011092101a032a93a388b79e99e69c2d5c870
Copying blob sha256:26fac3e9314ebcc28feb6049f712a878479f900bc5f70456ecc2fc2fa3695d30
Copying blob sha256:6d2b9ea538182f9ba83b0f571df811bbc4de7f25b42ee9eb9733ce9d5851970f
Copying blob sha256:ea44d309ec90a992e100ebb714b75633eea050267a3070b3db27c666fae34be2
Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:f18232174bc91741fdf3da96d85011092101a032a93a388b79e99e69c2d5c870"/""/"sha256:08000c18d16dadf9553d747a58cf44023423a9ab010aab96cf263d2216b8b350": unpacking failed (error: exit status 125; output: Error: unrecognized command `podman /`

Did you mean this?
	cp
	ps
	rm

Try 'podman --help' for more information
)

### Describe the results you expected

Correct image processing

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Happens all the time and in our code at the FROM statement in our main project.  The reproducer/simplified Dockerfile provided here is able to get past the FROM statement but the failure error is the same at the podman pull in the reproducer.

comments.body:
When running podman from inside a container (having podman 5.3.2 inside the container) our use case fails at the FROM statement:

sh -c podman buildx build --arch amd64 --os linux \
-f Dockerfile.build \
--no-cache \
-t xyz.jfrog.io/docker-local/xyz-tomcat:9.0.104-alpine.0 \
.
STEP 1/34: FROM --platform=linux/amd64 eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine
Resolving "eclipse-temurin" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:26fac3e9314ebcc28feb6049f712a878479f900bc5f70456ecc2fc2fa3695d30
Copying blob sha256:6d2b9ea538182f9ba83b0f571df811bbc4de7f25b42ee9eb9733ce9d5851970f
Copying blob sha256:ea44d309ec90a992e100ebb714b75633eea050267a3070b3db27c666fae34be2
Copying blob sha256:994661c98e4264bf53af5a49e1f142717665bd41915c9244d1f63dad7897149f
Copying blob sha256:f18232174bc91741fdf3da96d85011092101a032a93a388b79e99e69c2d5c870
Error: creating build container: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:f18232174bc91741fdf3da96d85011092101a032a93a388b79e99e69c2d5c870"/""/"sha256:08000c18d16dadf9553d747a58cf44023423a9ab010aab96cf263d2216b8b350": unpacking failed (error: exit status 125; output: Error: unrecognized command `podman /`

Did you mean this?
        cp
        ps
        rm

Try 'podman --help' for more information
)

Are you trying to run an x86_64 container on aarch64 vai emulation. Podman/buildah cannot be run in that way due to how the emulation messes with argv[0] which we need for proper reexec see https://github.com/containers/buildah/discussions/5322
